headline: CONFIRMATION with NEGATIVE-PRESSURE FLAG. April 7-21 Mythos Preview unauthorized access incident is the most significant new in-window evidence: Anthropic released the first model it publicly designated 'too dangerous for public release' under Project Glasswing, then suffered a Discord-group breach within ~14 hours via guessable endpoint URL + improperly-scoped vendor credentials inherited from a prior Mercor data leak. TechCrunch (Apr 21), Bloomberg (Apr 21), Cybernews, Fortune (Apr 24) confirm the breach occurred without sophisticated technique — it was effectively predictable operational hygiene failure. This is a SUBSTANTIVE negative SYS/ACC signal partially offset by Anthropic's voluntary acknowledgment of the breach and ongoing investigation transparency. Net composite delta is sub-5pt: the Glasswing program itself (controlled-distribution-to-defenders model, voluntary too-dangerous-to-publicly-release classification) remains a positive AWR/INT structural choice, and the April 22 96-page DC Circuit filing (already absorbed yesterday) reinforces INT/ACC. Composite 61.6 (Established) holds with elevated negative pressure on SYS subscore.

evidence.finding: Discord group obtained unauthorized access to Mythos Preview ~14 hours after April 7 Project Glasswing launch via guessed endpoint URL using naming-convention knowledge from prior Mercor leak combined with active vendor contractor credentials. No exploit code or social engineering required.
Anthropic confirmed investigation; said no evidence Anthropic systems compromised; breach attributed to third-party vendor environment.
Mythos autonomously discovered thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in pre-release testing; Discord group access could shrink defender-vs-attacker patch window. SUBSTANTIVE downstream BND signal.
Anthropic investigating; Project Glasswing premise (controlled distribution to defenders) compromised within first day of operation.

Project Glasswing launched April 7 with $100M usage credits to 50+ orgs; Mythos publicly designated 'too dangerous for public release' — voluntary safety-tier classification is positive AWR/INT structural choice.
Confirms Anthropic publicly framed Mythos as too dangerous for general release — first such voluntary designation by major lab.
Routine election-safeguards update — incremental positive AWR/SYS signal not sufficient to move composite.

negativeSignals: Mythos breach is the strongest negative signal in window — operational security failure at the precise moment Anthropic chose to release a self-designated dangerous capability. Third-party vendor scoping is the proximate cause; this maps to SYS (operational risk management) and ACC (access controls). Composite math: SYS 3.8 → conservatively could trend toward 3.6-3.7 if the breach narrative deepens with new evidence; not sufficient on its own to cross 5pt threshold given offsetting positives.
positiveSignals: (1) Self-imposed too-dangerous-to-publicly-release designation is unprecedented voluntary AWR/INT signal. (2) Public investigation acknowledgment and transparency. (3) DC Circuit filing reinforces refusal to permit mass surveillance / autonomous weapons. (4) Election safeguards update.
